name: Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail
name_original: El Monte de las Cenizas (Circular GR 92 & PR-MU 2)
region: National Park  Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila
description: Hike the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail, a difficult 7.9-mile route with panoramic views, ancient Roman roads, and historic military batter
region_intro: Hiking around National Park Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila offers diverse landscapes along the Murcia coastline. The region features untouched beaches, secluded coves, and dramatic cliffs, alongside mountainous terrain such as Monte de las Cenizas (307m) and Peña del Águila. Geological formations include fossil dunes and sedimentary rocks, while wetlands provide habitats for various bird species. The park's varied environments support a rich flora and fauna, making it a botanical jewel. Best easy hiking trails around National Park Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila The most popular easy hiking route is Punta Negra Viewpoint – Cala de los Déntoles loop from Cala Arturo, a 3.6 miles (5.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and access to secluded coves. Another top favourite among local hikers is Viewpoint of Portman Bay – Cenizas Coastal Battery loop from Atamaria, an easy 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This trail includes views of Portman Bay and the historic Las Cenizas military battery. Local hikers also love the Portman Lighthouse – Portman Lighthouse loop from Villa Romana del Paturro, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through coastal scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 9 minutes. Hiking around National Park Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila is defined by diverse coastal environments, mountainous terrain, and wetlands. The network offers options for various ability levels, with 22 easy hiking trails available. The routes in National Park Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 470 reviews. More than 2,700 hikers have used komoot to explore National Park Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are there in National Park Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila?
A: There are 22 easy hiking trails available in National Park Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila, offering diverse landscapes along the Murcia coastline.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on easy hikes in National Park Calblanque?
A: Easy hikes in the park feature a rich variety of landscapes, including untouched beaches, secluded coves, and dramatic cliffs. Inland, you'll find mountainous formations like Monte de las Cenizas and Peña del Águila, as well as wetlands and salt flats such as the Humedal de las Salinas de Rasall, which is excellent for birdwatching.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in National Park Calblanque?
A: The park is a protected natural area, and while specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the diverse coastal and mountainous terrain suggests pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn, avoiding the peak heat of summer. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures for exploring the trails and enjoying the flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or viewpoints along easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, several easy trails offer access to notable landmarks and viewpoints. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4460935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chapa Battery and Portmán Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7048648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Cenizas&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Fuente&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent vistas.

Q: Can I explore historical sites on easy hikes in the park?
A: Absolutely. Beyond its natural beauty, the park holds historical significance. You can find archaeological remains from Neolithic to Roman times and evidence of past mining activity. Military batteries like La Chapa and Las Cenizas are also accessible, offering insights into the area's strategic past and beautiful views.

Q: Are there easy circular walks available in National Park Calblanque?
A: Yes, there are several easy circular walks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19387479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Negra Viewpoint – Cala de los Déntoles loop from Cala Arturo&lt;/a&gt; offers coastal views and access to secluded coves. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6891955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portman Lighthouse – Portman Lighthouse loop from Villa Romana del Paturro&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through coastal scenery.

Q: Is National Park Calblanque accessible by public transport for hikers?
A: While the region research mentions the park is a protected natural area, specific public transport options directly to trailheads for easy hikes are not detailed. It's advisable to check local transport schedules or consider driving to access the various starting points within the park.

Q: Where can I find parking for easy hikes in National Park Calblanque?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19387479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Negra Viewpoint – Cala de los Déntoles loop&lt;/a&gt;, you would typically start from Cala Arturo. For the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27634697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Portman Bay – Cenizas Coastal Battery loop&lt;/a&gt;, the starting point is Atamaria. It is recommended to check specific route details for parking information near your chosen trailhead.

Q: Are the easy trails in National Park Calblanque suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the easy trails are suitable for families, offering manageable distances and gentle elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21853778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from Playa Larga – Playa Larga Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter option at 3.8 km with minimal elevation, making it a good choice for families.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the easy hiking trails in National Park Calblanque?
A: The park's regulations regarding dogs on trails are not explicitly detailed in the provided information. As a protected natural area, it's always best to check local signage or the official park website for current rules on pet access to ensure compliance and protect the local wildlife.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in National Park Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 470 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse coastal environments, the stunning sea views, and the opportunity to explore both untouched beaches and historical military batteries. The variety of landscapes and well-maintained trails are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any easy trails that offer views of Portman Bay?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27634697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Portman Bay – Cenizas Coastal Battery loop from Atamaria&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.6 km path that specifically includes views of Portman Bay and the historic Las Cenizas military battery.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to secluded coves or beaches?
A: Absolutely. The park is known for its diverse coastal environments, including untouched beaches and secluded coves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19387479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Negra Viewpoint – Cala de los Déntoles loop from Cala Arturo&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, offering coastal views and direct access to hidden coves.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the trail is located near Portman within the Natural Park of Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas, and Peña del Águila. Look for designated parking at trailheads or near the start of the loop in the Portman area. Public transport options are generally limited, so driving is often the most convenient way to reach the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it's located within a regional park, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Remember to bring enough water for both you and your dog, especially since there are no shops along the route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). During these periods, the temperatures are more pleasant for hiking. Summer can be very hot, making the trail more challenging. Early mornings are also recommended to avoid the midday heat and enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the regional park?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no permits or entrance fees required to access the Natural Park of Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas, and Peña del Águila, where this trail is located. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially during peak seasons, as access restrictions or specific rules might apply to certain areas within the park.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks and viewpoints will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. You'll enjoy Coastal views from the Parque Regional Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas and Peña del Águila, and panoramic vistas from the Viewpoint of Portman Bay. A major highlight is the Batería de las Cenizas Viewpoint, featuring impressive 38.1 cm coastal defense guns and a unique Mayan-inspired concrete entrance. You'll also walk along a well-preserved ancient Roman road for a couple of kilometers.

faqs.question: Is the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While some sources describe sections as easy to moderate, the full loop is rated as difficult on komoot, covering over 12 km with significant elevation gain. Beginners with good fitness might manage, but it's advisable to be prepared for varied terrain, including steep ascents. There are easier, shorter sections within the park if you prefer a less challenging experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this loop trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll traverse through dense forests of Aleppo pines and other Mediterranean flora, encounter sinuous forest tracks, and experience steep sections, particularly on the ascent to the summit. A unique part of the trail includes walking on a well-preserved ancient Roman road. Expect a mix of natural paths and some wider tracks.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: The full Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail, which is approximately 12.7 km long, typically takes around 4 hours and 30 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace, fitness level, and how much time you spend enjoying the viewpoints and historical sites along the way.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find yourself on sections of the Sendero del Mediterráneo - Murcia, Monte Cenizas, Monte de las cenizas, Llano del Beal, Transmurciana 05, Cenizas, Calzada Romana, and Peña del Águila trails, offering opportunities to explore further if you wish.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and difficulty, it's essential to bring comfortable, sturdy hiking shoes, plenty of water (as there are no shops along the route), and snacks. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also recommended, especially during sunnier months. A map or GPS device is useful for navigation, and don't forget your camera for the stunning views!

faqs.question: Is the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail family-friendly?
faqs.answer: While some shorter, easier sections within the park might be suitable for families, the full 12.7 km loop is rated as difficult and involves significant elevation changes. It might be too challenging for very young children or those unaccustomed to longer hikes. Consider your family's fitness level before attempting the entire loop.

waypoints.name: Coastal views from the Parque Regional Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas and Peña del Águila
